title: Cooking Oil on Linen
stainChemistry: Cooking oil on linen: thin triglycerides on cellulose fiber. Linen is more absorbent than cotton — oil penetrates fast and weave is open. Standard powder preload + Dawn protocol works on simple flat linen. Linen-specific cautions: hot water shrinks aggressively, dyed linen bleeds, structured pieces (linen suits, lined dresses, table runners) warp under wet treatment. Polyunsaturated oils polymerize fastest under heat.
whyThisWorks: Cornstarch / fuller's earth pulls bulk oil up via capillary on linen's open weave. Dawn handles residual oil. Cool wash + air-dry flat preserves linen shape. Honest fork: simple linen napkin / shirt = home protocol; structured / vintage / dry-clean-only = pro.
defaultAssumption: Simple linen napkin / shirt = home protocol. Structured / lined / trimmed / vintage / dry-clean-only = pro from minute one.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Dry-clean-only label = pro. Structured / lined / trimmed / vintage = pro. Simple flat linen = proceed.
Cover oil spot with thick layer of powder. 20–30 min absorb. Brush off softly.
Dawn in cool water, soft toothbrush 5–10 min. Cool rinse.
Cool wash per care label — NEVER hot (linen shrinks). Air-DRY FLAT — never tumble-dry until fully clear.

steps: 1. Care label + structure check. Structured = pro.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — linen shrinks under hot
warnings: NEVER hot water — linen shrinks aggressively
NEVER tumble-dry until fully clear — heat shrinks + sets oil
Polyunsaturated oils polymerize fastest under heat

homeSolutions.agent: Structured Linen Goes to Pro; Simple Linen Stays Home
homeSolutions.instruction: Linen has two cases. SIMPLE LINEN (napkin, basic shirt, plain skirt) = standard powder + Dawn protocol works. STRUCTURED LINEN (suits, lined dresses, tablecloths with stiffeners, dry-clean-only) = pro from minute one. Wet treatment on structured linen risks shape change.
Cover oil with cornstarch, baking soda, or fuller's earth (works best on oil). 20 to 30 minutes. Soft brush off.
Mix 1 tablespoon Dawn in 2 cups cool water. Work into the spot with a soft toothbrush 5–10 min. Cool rinse.
Cool wash per care label — NEVER hot (linen shrinks). AIR-DRY FLAT — do NOT tumble-dry until fully clear. Heat shrinks linen + sets residual oil.

steps: 1. Structured / lined / trimmed = pro. Simple linen = home.
2. NO hot water. NO chlorine bleach.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if linen is structured, hot water / dryer was applied previously, or oil is aged + polymerized.

neverDo: Never wet-treat structured linen at home
fiberSensitivities: Linen shrinks aggressively under hot water
Structured linen warps under wet treatment

escalation.when: Structured / lined / trimmed linen, dry-clean-only label, hot water / dryer used previously, vintage piece, 3 home rounds plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Cooking oil on [simple / structured / dyed / vintage] linen, age [hours / days / weeks], home-treated with powder preload + Dawn + cool wash + air-dry flat. No hot water / no chlorine bleach. [Residual oily feel / structured concerns]."

contextQuestions: Type of oil — olive / canola / vegetable / peanut?
Has it been through a hot wash or dryer?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Vintage linen needs cool everything. Structured linen needs professional finishing. Polyunsaturated oils polymerize fastest under heat — hot-dried = often permanent.
